claims.claim_text: Kubernetes makes the contract-and-reconciler pattern familiar because users submit resources to the API and controllers work to match actual state to desired state.
claims.span_hint: section: 5. Self-Service Needs A Contract And A Reconciler, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes object model docs directly describe desired state, current state, and the control plane actively managing actual state to match the declared state.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes controllers run reconciliation loops, with controllers moving current cluster state toward desired state.
claims.rationale: The controllers concept page explicitly defines controllers as control loops and says each controller tries to move current state closer to desired state.

claims.claim_text: The Kubernetes Pod Security Standards define three policy levels: Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted.
claims.rationale: The Pod Security Standards page directly states that the standard defines three policies and names them.

claims.claim_text: OpenTelemetry treats traces, metrics, and logs as separate telemetry signals.
claims.rationale: The OpenTelemetry signals page explicitly lists traces, metrics, and logs as supported signal categories.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Controllers
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary reference for reconciliation loops, controller behavior, and desired-versus-current state.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Pod Security Standards
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful background for the module's guardrail and hard-control examples in shared clusters.

further_reading.title: OpenTelemetry Signals
further_reading.why_relevant: Directly supports the module's observability discussion by defining traces, metrics, and logs as distinct signals.

notes: Most of the module is pedagogical reasoning, quiz material, or workflow advice and does not need inline citations. Code blocks, tables, quiz prompts/options, and generic platform-opinion prose were excluded; the main inline-citation needs are the explicit Kubernetes/OpenTelemetry standards statements and the Kubernetes desired-state/reconciliation sentence.
